compiled_state.title: Mutation testing: identify and prune change-detector tests
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
The suite grew via characterize→port→parity during the migration and likely carries change-detector tests (tautological, assertion-light, or pinned-to-implementation rather than behavior) that resist refactoring without catching bugs.

## What
- Stand up mutation testing (e.g. mutmut or cosmic-ray) over src/rebar core modules; record baseline mutation score.
- Identify surviving mutants (untested behavior) AND change-detector tests (tests that pass under mutation / assert nothing meaningful / restate the implementation).
- Prune or rewrite change-detector tests to assert observable behavior; add tests to kill high-value surviving mutants.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Mutation testing wired (documented invocation) with a recorded baseline score
- [ ] Change-detector tests catalogued and pruned/rewritten to behavioral assertions
- [ ] High-value surviving mutants killed; mutation score improved and recorded
- [ ] Suite still green locally + CI

comments.body: mutmut 3.6 wired into /tmp/rebar-dev with committed [tool.mutmut] config (source_paths=src/rebar, only_mutate scoped to signing/gates/next_batch/validate/_processors; fast fail-fast runner over the dev venv). Trial run on signing.py: 491 mutants, 371 killed, 10 survived (all equivalent: utf-8 alias / error-string / temp-prefix mutations), rest CLI arms unmapped by coverage. Scaling to the 5-module core now.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Baseline mutation run complete (5-module core, 3796 mutants, 22.9 mut/s). Per-module mapped scores: signing 99.2% (3 survivors, all equivalent), reducer/_processors 97.3% (19 survivors), next_batch 62.7% (236 survivors), gates 100% / validate 100% over mapped (large no-tests counts because their interface tests drive via subprocess, coverage-blind). Root cause of next_batch survivors: the only IN-PROCESS test (test_library_and_mcp_shape) asserts ~nothing (batch_size==2 + schema). Remediating: rich in-process behavioral tests for compute/to_json_dict (output keys+values) and reducer REVERT/BRIDGE_ALERT/VERIFY_COMMANDS record fields.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Remediation complete. next_batch 62.7%->99.2% (survivors 236->6), reducer/_processors 97.3%->99.4% (19->5). Rewrote change-detector test_library_and_mcp_shape (was batch_size==2+schema only) to assert epic id/title, exact batch-item dicts, skip buckets. Added tests/interfaces/test_next_batch_behavior.py (13 in-process tests over compute/to_json_dict/render_*) and tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_record_fields.py (10 tests pinning REVERT/BRIDGE_ALERT/VERIFY_COMMANDS record fields). Remaining survivors (6 next_batch matrix-spacing, 5 _processors unused-param/dead-fallback) are equivalent/cosmetic, documented. Config in pyproject [tool.mutmut], doc at docs/mutation-testing.md. Full suite: 1749 passed/9 skipped (broad) + 8 passed (integration); ruff check+format clean. NOT committed (orchestrator commits).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: DONE. mutmut 3.6.0 wired ([tool.mutmut] in pyproject, dev-venv runner, docs/mutation-testing.md with recorded baseline). next_batch.py mapped score 62.7%->99.2% (survivors 236->6); reducer/_processors.py 97.3%->99.4%. Rewrote the assertion-light next_batch compute test to assert the full batch-item contract; added tests/interfaces/test_next_batch_behavior.py (13) + tests/scripts/reducer/test_reducer_record_fields.py (10) to kill high-value survivors. Remaining survivors documented as equivalents. Suite green (broad 1749 + integration 8); ruff clean. Implemented + independently reviewed (opus reviewer: APPROVE-WITH-NITS, confirmed via intentional-mutation kill checks). Commit 369d82ea.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
